description_long: This object consists of two distinct hammers, each with a robust wooden handle and a heavy metal head. The larger hammer features a double-faced sledge head, with both ends rounded and designed for delivering powerful blows, while the handle is thick, slightly curved, and darkened from use. The smaller hammer has a cross-peen head, with one flat striking face and a wedge-shaped peen opposite, suitable for metalworking or precision tasks. Both handles show a natural wood grain, with some visible wear and darkening, indicating frequent handling. The metal heads are dark, likely forged steel, and exhibit a matte, slightly rough finish with minor surface imperfections. No visible branding, logos, or additional attachments are present on either hammer.
description: A pair of well-used hammers with wooden handles and dark metal heads, one being a double-faced sledge and the other a cross-peen type. Both show signs of wear and have a rugged, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both hammers side by side, clearly displaying the double-faced sledge head and the cross-peen head with their wooden handles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the handles and the profile of the hammer heads, especially the wedge shape of the cross-peen.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the heads and handles visible, but from the opposite side, showing the symmetry of the sledge and the peen of the smaller hammer.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the rounded ends of the sledge and the narrow profile of the cross-peen head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, smooth but darkened from use, no added grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: One double-faced sledge head with rounded ends, and one cross-peen head with a flat striking face and a wedge-shaped peen.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible wear on handles, minor surface imperfections and darkening on metal heads, but no major damage.
